narrowness_statement: D-002K is funding-liquidity ONLY. No contagion / balance-sheet / cross-asset sources are bound. Narrowing scope against the inherited effect_too_small axis is legitimate design; loosening statistics at fixed scope would be laundering and is forbidden. D-002J remains terminally REFUSED at P7.

forbidden_use: not pre-2018 repo history (use LIBOR/OIS instead, out of D-002K scope)
observables.evidence_notes: SOFR is the canonical point-in-time overnight secured funding rate; BIS QR 2019 Q4 anchors the 2019 US repo spike on SOFR percentiles. Published next business day, not revised; release_date == observation_date+1bd boundary still enforced.

observables.series_reference: H.15 SOFR vs EFFR/OBFR spread (TGCR/BGCR/SOFR post-2014 block)
forbidden_use: not post-LIBOR continuity without explicit methodology note
not credit-spread interpretation (this is a funding basis, not a credit spread)
observables.evidence_notes: H.15 secured-vs-unsecured overnight rate spread is the classic funding-liquidity stress widening signal. FRED mirrors H.15 under DGS/DFF/etc.; primary discipline uses the H.15 release boundary.

observables.series_reference: OFR Short-Term Funding Monitor tri-party haircut distribution / dealer-to-money-fund volume
forbidden_use: not bilateral uncleared repo segments in detail
not European repo markets (out of OFR coverage and D-002K scope)
observables.evidence_notes: OFR tri-party haircut dispersion / dealer-money-fund volume captures the funding-liquidity volatility burst during the 2019 repo spike. Methodology revisions documented; revision-stable monitor series, release boundary enforced.

observables.series_reference: OFR FSI funding subcomponent (ofr_fsi_credit_equity_safe_assets_funding_volatility -> funding leg)
forbidden_use: not used as a y-label when constructed from the same underlying series as x
not the full composite OFR-FSI (only the funding subcomponent is in D-002K scope)
observables.evidence_notes: Only the FUNDING subcomponent of OFR-FSI is bound (not the composite). Recovery_time = elapsed time for the funding leg to return to pre-window baseline band; Monin 2017 documents construction. Funding-liquidity relevant only.

observables.series_reference: SOFR 99th percentile daily series (tail of secured overnight distribution)
forbidden_use: not intra-day intra-segment dynamics (lost in daily aggregate)
observables.evidence_notes: SOFR 99th-percentile slope captures funding-liquidity transition steepness (how abruptly the secured-rate tail jumps). The 2019 spike pushed the SOFR 99th pct sharply; daily-aggregate limitation acknowledged.

observables.series_reference: STLFSI4 composite US financial-stress index (funding-stress proxy, weekly)
forbidden_use: not a continuous series across the 2022 LIBOR break without methodology note
observables.evidence_notes: STLFSI is a revisable constructed index whose constituents are re-estimated; vintage_required=true so point-in-time STLFSI vintages (release_date) must be used, NOT post-hoc revised values. Stress_persistence = number of consecutive periods above the pre-window stress band. Kliesen-Smith 2010 construction.

observables.series_reference: ALFRED real-time vintages of the FRED TEDRATE / OIS-Treasury funding-spread proxy (point-in-time)
forbidden_use: not post-hoc revised data used for in-window prediction
not a non-FRED-native series (ALFRED only vintages FRED-native series)
observables.evidence_notes: ALFRED supplies the point-in-time vintage lens for the revisable funding-spread proxy used in the CW5 UK gilt/LDI window. vintage_required=true: the D-002I/D-002J look-ahead lesson applied at the observable layer (Croushore 2011 requires ALFRED for honest replication). PARTIAL audit status acknowledged; vintage alignment with decision_date is explicit, not assumed.

observables.series_reference: VIXCLS (CBOE VIX close via FRED) -- CONTROL COVARIATE ONLY, NOT a funding-liquidity observable
forbidden_use: not a funding-liquidity observable (it is a market-wide equity-vol control only)
not a Kuramoto-style coherence observable; cross-asset coherence does not prove interbank contagion
observables.evidence_notes: VIX is explicitly a CONTROL covariate, NOT a funding-liquidity observable. It conditions / de-confounds market-wide risk-off but never feeds the funding-liquidity signal as an endpoint. role=control_covariate enforced by test. Echoes the cross-asset != interbank discipline.
